llm_operation.reason: PaddlePaddle baddbmm does not support float16 on CPU per error message; only float32/float64 are supported. PyTorch supports float16, but to enable cross-framework comparison, we must align dtype to Paddle's supported types. Repair by changing dtype to float32 in both frameworks.

PyTorch torch.baddbmm expects 'batch1' and 'batch2' as positional args, not 'x' and 'y'; also 'beta' and 'alpha' are keyword-only in PyTorch but positional in Paddle; mismatched parameter binding causes functional inconsistency. Must align arg names: use 'batch1'/'batch2' for PyTorch and keep 'x'/'y' for Paddle, and ensure beta/alpha passed as kwargs in PyTorch.

PyTorch's torch.baddbmm expects batch1 and batch2 as the matrices to multiply, while Paddle's paddle.baddbmm uses x and y. The original test cases use identical shapes but do not ensure equivalent numerical inputs — both frameworks must use the same random or deterministic values for input/x/batch1 and y/batch2 to compare correctly. Also, PyTorch requires explicit out_dtype only in specific CUDA cases, but mismatched tensor values (not just shapes) cause large numerical divergence (>13). Repair by adding consistent 'value' fields to all tensor specs to guarantee identical initialization.
